prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins in a domestic interior, but the first sampled frame is blurred and appears to include a dissolve or superimposed image. A child or young person is seated at a table in the foreground with a bowl or dish nearby. Behind them, an adult stands near a counter or cabinet. The room includes household furnishings such as curtains or a window on the left, shelving/cupboards in the back, and a rail-like structure on the right. Bright circular, wheel-like shapes are also visible as a transparent overlay, but their source is unclear from the frame.

The scene then changes to a black-and-white close-up of a rectangular metal baking pan placed on a plain surface. The pan appears to contain a flat layer of batter or baked mixture, with a darker area near one corner. A vertical “TTC MEDIA” watermark is visible along the right edge.

By the final frame, the food item has become a finished round cake displayed on a plate or stand. The cake is covered with thick, glossy icing or glaze that drips down the sides. A large wedge has been cut out, exposing a dark, dense interior crumb. A few crumbs are visible on the plate in front of the cut section. The same vertical “TTC MEDIA” watermark remains on the right side.
